skill.name: monorepo-architect
skill.description: Guides monorepo architecture, build systems, dependency management, caching, and CI optimization for multi-project software teams.
skill.summary: Plan, migrate, and optimize monorepos with practical build and dependency guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and two weak cryptography patterns. Review found no command execution, cryptographic implementation, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Backtick Reference
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 20. The line only uses Markdown inline code for a local document path and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains a documentation reference inside Markdown backticks. No shell, Ruby, subprocess, or executable instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography Text Match
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. The line is a plain language skill description and contains no cryptographic code or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is prose in front matter. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or security-sensitive implementation appears on the cited line.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Workflow Text Match
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 45. The line describes designing workspace structure and does not reference cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a workflow step about project structure. It provides no evidence of weak cryptography or unsafe security behavior.

content.value_statement: Large codebases often suffer from slow builds, unclear ownership, and duplicated tooling. This skill helps teams choose structure, tooling, caching, and CI patterns for scalable monorepos.
actual_capabilities: Compares Nx, Turborepo, Bazel, and Lerna for multi-project repositories.
Recommends workspace layouts and project boundaries for shared code.
Plans local and remote build caching strategies.
Designs affected-change detection for faster CI pipelines.
Advises on dependency graph rules, tags, and ownership conventions.
Outlines migration steps from separate repositories to a monorepo.
limitations: It provides architectural guidance, not a complete automated migration.
It depends on accurate details about teams, repositories, and build tools.
It does not replace performance testing in the target CI environment.
It may need project-specific follow-up for uncommon build systems.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Workspace
use_cases.description: Create a practical monorepo structure with clear project boundaries, shared libraries, and build tool choices.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow CI
use_cases.description: Identify caching, affected detection, and task pipeline changes that reduce wasted build and test time.

use_cases.title: Migrate From Polyrepo
use_cases.description: Map existing repositories into a staged monorepo migration with ownership and dependency rules.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Monorepo Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Nx, Turborepo, Bazel, and Lerna for a repository with these apps, libraries, languages, and CI constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a monorepo workspace structure for these teams and projects. Include project boundaries, shared libraries, ownership, and naming conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Build Pipelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this monorepo build workflow and propose local caching, remote caching, affected detection, and parallel task improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged migration plan from these separate repositories into a monorepo. Include dependency cleanup, CI changes, risks, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A team has three web apps, two shared libraries, and slow pull request builds.
output_examples.output: A recommended workspace layout, build pipeline sequence, affected-test strategy, and cache plan for faster pull request feedback.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants to migrate ten services from separate repositories.
output_examples.output: A phased migration roadmap with repository grouping, ownership rules, dependency constraints, and rollback checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A repository uses shared code but has unclear dependency boundaries.
output_examples.output: A dependency graph review plan with tagging rules, library ownership guidance, and enforcement recommendations.

best_practices: Provide repository size, languages, CI provider, and team ownership before asking for architecture advice.
Start with project boundaries and dependency rules before choosing advanced caching settings.
Validate recommendations with real build metrics and pull request feedback times.
anti_patterns: Do not merge repositories before defining ownership, naming, and dependency rules.
Do not enable remote caching without a clear cache key and security model.
Do not treat shared libraries as a dumping ground for unrelated code.

faq.question: Which monorepo tools does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Nx, Turborepo, Bazel, and Lerna at an architectural and workflow level.

faq.question: Can it generate a complete migration script?
faq.answer: No. It can plan migration steps, but implementation depends on the repository and tooling details.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for small teams?
faq.answer: Yes, when small teams manage multiple apps or libraries and need consistent tooling.

faq.question: Can it help reduce CI time?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest affected detection, task orchestration, parallel execution, and caching strategies.

faq.question: Does it require a specific programming language?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is mostly language-independent, though tool recommendations depend on the stack.

faq.question: Can it review an existing monorepo?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the structure, build commands, dependency rules, and CI workflow for a focused review.
